title: MarginalizationAK
lean_code_with_comments:
/-- Predicate "every intermediate vertex of the walk `π` lies
in `W`". For `π : Walk G v w`, this says
`∀ x ∈ π.support.tail.dropLast, x ∈ W`, i.e. the vertex sequence
`v_1, …, v_{n-1}` (with the start and end vertices excluded) is
contained in `W`. Length-0 and length-1 walks have empty
interiors, so the predicate holds vacuously. Mirrors the LN's
"$w_1, \dots, w_{n-1} \in W$ (if any)" clause in
`graphs.tex` def_3_14 items iii and iv. -/
def InteriorIn {v w : α} (π : Walk G v w) (W : Set α) : Prop :=
  ∀ x ∈ π.support.tail.dropLast, x ∈ W

/-- The *marginalization* of the CDMG `G` with respect to a set
of output nodes `W ⊆ α`: the new CDMG `G^{\sm W}` obtained by
deleting the nodes of `W` (`V^{\sm W} := G.V \ W`, inputs
unchanged) and replacing every walk through `W` by a single
edge -- a directed edge in `E^{\sm W}` if the walk is directed,
a bidirected edge in `L^{\sm W}` if the walk is a bifurcation.
See `lecture-notes/lecture_notes/graphs.tex` definition
`def:G_marginalization` (def_3_14 of the LN).

This is intentionally well-defined for *every* `W : Set α`,
with no `W ⊆ G.V` precondition -- see the design note above for
why (iteration / composition cleanliness, mirroring
`hardInterventionOn`). The four `@[simp]` lemmas
`marginalize_J`, `marginalize_V`, `mem_marginalize_E`,
`mem_marginalize_L` below characterise the four components of
the result.

The `L^{\sm W}` membership condition takes the bifurcation
existential in *both* walk directions (the LN's symmetric
"between" reading) and excludes pairs already in `E^{\sm W}` in
either direction (Lean-encoding-specific, see the design note
on `disjoint_EL`). Downstream claims that quantify over
bifurcation *existence* (e.g. claim_3_16) should also use the
symmetric reading. -/
def marginalize (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α) : CDMG α where
  J := G.J
  V := G.V \ W
  disjoint_JV := by
    rw [Set.disjoint_left]
    rintro x hJ ⟨hV, _⟩
    exact Set.disjoint_left.mp G.disjoint_JV hJ hV
  E := { p : α × α |
    p.1 ∈ G.J ∪ (G.V \ W) ∧ p.2 ∈ G.V \ W ∧
    ∃ π : Walk G p.1 p.2, π.IsDirected ∧ π.InteriorIn W ∧ 1 ≤ π.length }
  E_subset := by
    rintro ⟨u, v⟩ ⟨hu, hv, _⟩
    exact ⟨hu, hv⟩
  L := { p : α × α |
    p.1 ∈ G.V \ W ∧ p.2 ∈ G.V \ W ∧ p.1 ≠ p.2 ∧
    (¬ ∃ π : Walk G p.1 p.2, π.IsDirected ∧ π.InteriorIn W) ∧
    (¬ ∃ π : Walk G p.2 p.1, π.IsDirected ∧ π.InteriorIn W) ∧
    ((∃ π : Walk G p.1 p.2, π.IsBifurcation ∧ π.InteriorIn W) ∨
     (∃ π : Walk G p.2 p.1, π.IsBifurcation ∧ π.InteriorIn W)) }
  L_subset := by
    rintro ⟨u, v⟩ ⟨hu, hv, _, _, _, _⟩
    exact ⟨hu, hv⟩
  L_irrefl := by
    intro v₁ v₂ h
    exact h.2.2.1
  L_symm := by
    intro v₁ v₂ h
    obtain ⟨hu, hv, hne, hnE_uv, hnE_vu, h_or⟩ := h
    exact ⟨hv, hu, hne.symm, hnE_vu, hnE_uv, h_or.symm⟩
  disjoint_EL := by
    rw [Set.disjoint_left]
    intro p hE hL
    obtain ⟨_, _, π, hπ_dir, hπ_int, _⟩ := hE
    obtain ⟨_, _, _, hnE_uv, _, _⟩ := hL
    exact hnE_uv ⟨π, hπ_dir, hπ_int⟩
lean_code_without_comments:
def InteriorIn {v w : α} (π : Walk G v w) (W : Set α) : Prop :=
  ∀ x ∈ π.support.tail.dropLast, x ∈ W

def marginalize (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α) : CDMG α where
  J := G.J
  V := G.V \ W
  disjoint_JV := by
    rw [Set.disjoint_left]
    rintro x hJ ⟨hV, _⟩
    exact Set.disjoint_left.mp G.disjoint_JV hJ hV
  E := { p : α × α |
    p.1 ∈ G.J ∪ (G.V \ W) ∧ p.2 ∈ G.V \ W ∧
    ∃ π : Walk G p.1 p.2, π.IsDirected ∧ π.InteriorIn W ∧ 1 ≤ π.length }
  E_subset := by
    rintro ⟨u, v⟩ ⟨hu, hv, _⟩
    exact ⟨hu, hv⟩
  L := { p : α × α |
    p.1 ∈ G.V \ W ∧ p.2 ∈ G.V \ W ∧ p.1 ≠ p.2 ∧
    (¬ ∃ π : Walk G p.1 p.2, π.IsDirected ∧ π.InteriorIn W) ∧
    (¬ ∃ π : Walk G p.2 p.1, π.IsDirected ∧ π.InteriorIn W) ∧
    ((∃ π : Walk G p.1 p.2, π.IsBifurcation ∧ π.InteriorIn W) ∨
     (∃ π : Walk G p.2 p.1, π.IsBifurcation ∧ π.InteriorIn W)) }
  L_subset := by
    rintro ⟨u, v⟩ ⟨hu, hv, _, _, _, _⟩
    exact ⟨hu, hv⟩
  L_irrefl := by
    intro v₁ v₂ h
    exact h.2.2.1
  L_symm := by
    intro v₁ v₂ h
    obtain ⟨hu, hv, hne, hnE_uv, hnE_vu, h_or⟩ := h
    exact ⟨hv, hu, hne.symm, hnE_vu, hnE_uv, h_or.symm⟩
  disjoint_EL := by
    rw [Set.disjoint_left]
    intro p hE hL
    obtain ⟨_, _, π, hπ_dir, hπ_int, _⟩ := hE
    obtain ⟨_, _, _, hnE_uv, _, _⟩ := hL
    exact hnE_uv ⟨π, hπ_dir, hπ_int⟩
lean_explanation:
The carrier `α : Type*` is the ambient vertex type, matching the LN's silent assumption that a CDMG is parameterised by some underlying carrier. The LN's $G = (J, V, E, L)$ becomes `G : CDMG α` and the LN's $W \subseteq V$ becomes a plain `W : Set α` (no subset precondition — see the design notes). Reading `G.marginalize W` aloud gives "the marginalization of `G` with respect to `W`", i.e. the LN's $G^{\sm W}$.

The auxiliary predicate `Walk.InteriorIn` slices a `Walk G v w` to its interior vertex list `π.support.tail.dropLast` — Lean's `support` is the concrete `List` of vertices traversed by the walk, `tail` drops the head $v_0$, and `dropLast` drops the last $v_n$, leaving exactly the LN's $w_1, \dots, w_{n-1}$ (and automatically the empty list whenever the walk has length ≤ 1). The predicate `π.InteriorIn W := ∀ x ∈ π.support.tail.dropLast, x ∈ W` is therefore precisely the LN's "$w_1, \dots, w_{n-1} \in W$ (if any)" clause, with the parenthetical honoured by the vacuous empty-list case.

The definition itself is a `where`-block that supplies all ten fields of `CDMG α` in place: the four data fields `J`, `V`, `E`, `L` and the six structural obligations `disjoint_JV`, `E_subset`, `L_subset`, `L_irrefl`, `L_symm`, `disjoint_EL`. The LN's item iii becomes a set-comprehension on `E^{\sm W}`: source in `G.J ∪ (G.V \ W)`, target in `G.V \ W`, and a walk-existential `∃ π : Walk G p.1 p.2, π.IsDirected ∧ π.InteriorIn W ∧ 1 ≤ π.length`. Here `Walk` is the directional walk inductive of def_3_4, `π.IsDirected` says every step of `π` is `.forward`, and `π.IsBifurcation` (used in `L`) says the step sequence has the LN's $\hut\dots\hut\hus\tuh\dots\tuh$ shape (def_3_5). The LN's item iv becomes a set-comprehension on `L^{\sm W}` with both endpoints in `G.V \ W`, `p.1 ≠ p.2`, the bifurcation existential read in *both* walk directions (joined by `∨`), and two `¬ ∃ directed walk` exclusion clauses (see the design notes). Mathlib idioms in play: `\` denotes `Set.diff`, `×ˢ` denotes `Set.prod` (used implicitly inside the `E_subset` / `L_subset` obligations of `CDMG`), and `Set.disjoint_left` unfolds the `Disjoint` claims of `disjoint_JV` and `disjoint_EL` into elementwise non-membership.
design_choices:
**No `W ⊆ G.V` precondition.** The LN writes "let $W \subseteq V$", but the four-component formula is well-defined for every `W : Set α`. Vertices in `W \ G.V` never appear on any `Walk G _ _` (forced by `G.E_subset` / `G.L_subset`), so `π.InteriorIn W` is unaffected by the spurious part of `W`, and `G.V \ W` collapses to `G.V \ (W ∩ G.V)` — the same set the LN's restricted `W` produces. The payoff is iteration and composition: `(G.marginalize W₁).marginalize W₂` (claim_3_17) and the composition with `G.hardInterventionOn` (claim_3_18) become unconditional, matching the LN's own usage where the outer call's precondition is quietly suppressed.

**`1 ≤ π.length` in the directed-edge clause.** Without this length conjunct, the trivial `Walk.nil u : Walk G u u` — which is vacuously `IsDirected` and vacuously `InteriorIn W` (empty interior) — would force a universal self-loop `(u, u) ∈ E^{\sm W}` on every vertex, and `IsAcyclic` would no longer lift to the marginalization (falsifying claim_3_16 item 3 on acyclicity preservation). The LN's footnote "this may introduce self-cycles" refers to *genuine* cycles backed by a length-≥-1 walk through `W` (e.g. $u \to w \to u$ collapsing through $w \in W$), not to a trivial nil-walk on every vertex. For distinct endpoints the conjunct is free (any walk between them has length ≥ 1 structurally); the only behavioural effect is requiring a genuine cycle witness for self-edges, exactly as the LN's footnote intends.

**Bifurcation existential read in *both* walk directions.** In Lean, `Walk G u v` is directional and `Walk.IsBifurcation` is not symmetric: a single backward step `u ⟵[G] v` is a length-1 bifurcation of type `Walk G u v`, but its reversal is a forward step that is *not* a bifurcation (the LN's $\hus$ arrowhead-at-source hinge constraint fails on a single forward step). The LN's `L` is symmetric set-theoretically, so the natural "there exists a bifurcation between $u$ and $v$" reading has to be unrolled into both directions explicitly: `(∃ π : Walk G p.1 p.2, π.IsBifurcation ∧ π.InteriorIn W) ∨ (∃ π : Walk G p.2 p.1, π.IsBifurcation ∧ π.InteriorIn W)`. The alternative — prove that every bifurcation walk reverses to a bifurcation walk — fails on exactly the single-step backward case described above. As a bonus, the `L_symm` obligation becomes a one-line `Or.symm`.

**`L^{\sm W}` excludes pairs already in `E^{\sm W}` (in either direction).** The LN treats directed and bidirected edges as *different kinds* of objects (different ambient types), so a pair `(u, v)` being both directed and bidirected is fine in the LN's view. Our Lean encoding (def_3_1) collapses both into `Set (α × α)` for ergonomics and enforces literal set-disjointness via the `disjoint_EL` field. The marginalization can in principle produce collisions of this kind (a directed walk *and* a bifurcation through `W` between the same pair). We exclude such collisions from `L^{\sm W}` via two `¬ ∃ directed walk` clauses (in both walk directions, so the exclusion clause itself is symmetric in `p.1`, `p.2` and `L_symm` lifts trivially). The mathematical content is preserved at the level of bifurcation *existence*: a directed walk reverses to an all-`.backward` bifurcation walk, so the LN's symmetric "between" reading survives — provided downstream claims use the same symmetric convention, which matches the LN's own usage.

**`Walk.InteriorIn` slices the interior via `support.tail.dropLast`.** A walk's `support` is the concrete `List` $[v_0, v_1, \dots, v_n]$; dropping the head removes $v_0$ and dropping the last removes $v_n$, leaving exactly the LN's $w_1, \dots, w_{n-1}$ — and automatically `[]` whenever the walk has length ≤ 1, so the LN's "(if any)" parenthetical is honoured by the empty-list case. Two alternatives were considered and rejected: a bespoke `WalkInteriorIn` inductive type paralleling `Walk` with its own `nil` / `cons` constructors (parallel scaffolding where a `Prop` over the existing `support` list suffices), and `∀ x ∈ π.support, x ∈ W ∪ {v, w}` quantifying over the entire support with endpoint exceptions (equivalent but forces every call site to discharge the endpoint case-split explicitly). The chosen form is the idiomatic "internal-nodes-of-a-walk" pattern and composes cleanly with the `List.forall_mem_*` rewrites used downstream in claims 3.16 – 3.18.
